differences.title: 1. Reactivity
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS uses fine-grained reactivity with signals for efficient updates without a virtual DOM.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik achieves reactivity through its resumability model, minimizing client-side JavaScript execution.

differences.title: 2. Rendering
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS compiles JSX to efficient, imperative DOM updates.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik prioritizes lazy loading and serialization, avoiding a traditional Virtual DOM.

differences.title: 3. Initial Load
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS offers small bundle sizes due to its compilation approach.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik's resumability allows for instant interactivity without client-side hydration.

differences.title: 4. State Management
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS uses primitive signals and reactive stores for state.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik leverages context and props for state management across components.

differences.title: 5. Ecosystem
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS has a growing ecosystem with good community support.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik's ecosystem is newer, prioritizing performance-first solutions.

differences.title: 6. Learning Curve
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS uses JSX, familiar to React developers, with reactive primitives.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik introduces new concepts like resumability, requiring a learning investment.

differences.title: 7. Use Cases
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS excels in building performant, interactive UIs.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik is ideal for content-focused websites needing fast initial loads.
